what_it_does: This file is a ledger-style model card that records the identity, runtime status, architecture, size, tested loading, semantic-check results, launch pattern, quirks, open questions, and deployment rationale for the Hugging Face model jinaai/jina-embeddings-v5-text-small.
observations: Status is tested but not currently deployed
677M parameters, 1024 embedding dimensions, FP16, 1.3 GB disk, 8192-token native context
v5 architecture is natively supported by vLLM 0.21, while v4 is not
Semantic probe produced cat/feline 0.682 and cat/stocks 0.081
Positioned as a smaller, faster, instruction-aware alternative to BGE-M3
Currently not deployed because the embed slot is held by BGE-M3 due to multi-output capability
Provides a one-line swap command to replace the active embed model
Instruction-aware task instructions are prepended on the query side, not the document side
Open items include throughput, retrieval quality on Tim's corpora, and real-data generalisation of the probe separation
standouts: A tested vLLM embedding model card that includes explicit cosine-similarity probe numbers
Documents a runtime split: text-only/vLLM-served Jina v5 versus multimodal/standalone-served Jina v4
Records a model that is not deployed despite cleaner probe separation, with a concrete swap path
conventions: YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, dates, and tags
Wikilink-style internal references to related model cards and the models index
Section structure: At a glance, Source, Fits these needs, Tested performance, Known-good launch config, Quirks, Open at this entry, Connects to
Status labels and dated entries for provenance
concerns: The claim of sharper separation than BGE-M3 rests on a two-pair synthetic probe, not a retrieval benchmark
Status is tested even though concurrent throughput has not been measured
References Tim's actual corpora without defining who Tim is or which corpora
The multi-output capability tipping the default is asserted without quantified trade-offs
notes: A tested-but-idle vLLM embedding model card that explains why Jina v5 small is kept as a swappable alternative and how to activate it.
questions: What exact vLLM command and flags were used to load and pool the model?
What prompt template or instruction produced the reported cosine similarities?
What does BGE-M3 multi-output capability mean concretely for this substrate?
Who is Tim and what are the actual corpora for retrieval comparison?
What VRAM and concurrency budget would make deployment alongside other services safe?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document Jina v5 small as a tested alternative embedding model; the file matches that purpose by recording identity, config, probe results, quirks, and open questions.
apparent_themes: Ledger-style model inventory with tested status and deployment rationale
Runtime compatibility as a first-class architectural axis
Evidence-based candidate evaluation using simple semantic probes
Swap-in alternative design rather than a single fixed default
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to maintain an auditable, reusable record of an embedding option so the substrate can swap defaults based on compatibility, size, speed, and measured semantic discrimination, while clearly marking remaining evaluation gaps.
novelty: It explicitly captures a Jina v4/v5 runtime split and documents a tested-but-idle model together with a one-line swap command, treating deployment status as a mutable substrate decision rather than an intrinsic model property.
contribution: It contributes a self-contained model card with frontmatter identity, a vLLM launch pattern, semantic probe evidence, known limitations, and swap instructions, enabling later queries to compare embedding candidates on compatibility, size, and probe separation without relying on external sources.
summary_for_embedding: Model card for jinaai/jina-embeddings-v5-text-small, a 677M-parameter, 1024-dimensional, FP16 dense text embedding model served via vLLM --runner pooling, tested on 2026-05-26 with semantic probe similarities but not currently deployed.
intention_for_embedding: To record a tested, smaller, instruction-aware, vLLM-compatible embedding alternative and the conditions under which it could replace the current default.
